text: Micro scissors were also used to help further facilitate this micro dissection and separation from the thoracic spinal cord of the tumor.

Once the dural opening was achieved, we had the underlying thoracic meningioma well exposed and we started our dissection more laterally where the tumor originated from the inner surface of the dura matter.
We separated the tumor from the inside of the dura initially using sharp dissection as you can see here with an 11 blade.
Following this, because the tumor was relatively adherent to the inner surface of the dura, a CO2 laser was used to more finely separate the margins of the tumor from the inner surface of the dura.
